meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Learn how to generate passive income and overcome the 'can't finish projects' cycle. A tactical, intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To stop the cycle of unfinished projects, pivot toward building micro-SaaS tools that generate passive income. By leveraging AI-first workflows like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can shorten development cycles from months to days, creating a compounding loop of portfolio growth, technical mastery, and recurring revenue.
content.intro:
You know the graveyard: three half-baked clones, a forgotten React dashboard, and a database schema for an app that never saw a single user. The 'can't finish' syndrome isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of incentive. When you treat code as a theoretical exercise, your brain eventually checks out. 

True momentum begins when you stop building for 'experience' and start building for utility. By integrating passive income mechanisms into your workflow, you transform your portfolio from a list of abandoned repositories into a collection of revenue-generating assets. This shift changes the psychological stakes, turning every commit into a step toward financial independence.

steps.title: Define a Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a small, recurring pain point for a specific niche. Focus on a tool that provides a single, high-value output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following niche: [Insert Niche]. Generate a list of 5 'micro-SaaS' problems that can be solved with a single API call and a simple UI.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research top 10 pain points for [Niche] on Reddit and IndieHackers and output a JSON list of buildable ideas.'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Structure
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the project architecture, ensuring you avoid over-engineering and stay focused on the core feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal project structure for a [Project Type]. Include only essential dependencies and a clean folder architecture for rapid deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Scaffold a Next.js project with Supabase auth and Stripe integration using the directory structure defined by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Develop the primary value-add feature. This is the 'passive' part of the income—the engine that runs without your intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for [Feature]. Ensure the code is modular, error-handled, and ready for production-level execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the core logic in /src/lib/engine.ts ensuring type safety and robust error handling.'

steps.title: Automate the Monetization Layer
steps.description: Connect your application to Stripe or LemonSqueezy to enable seamless, automated payment collection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the server-side code for a Stripe subscription checkout session. Include webhook handling for successful payments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a secure API route for Stripe checkout sessions and a corresponding webhook handler to update user status in the database.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Ship your project to Vercel or Railway to ensure it is live, accessible, and ready for users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a production-ready configuration file for Vercel, including environment variable best practices and build optimization settings.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure project for production deployment and provide the steps to link the repository to Vercel.'

pro_tips: Always build the payment gateway before the UI; if you can't imagine someone paying for it, don't write the code.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you build; it prevents the 'it works on my machine' deployment nightmare.
Limit your MVP scope to 48 hours of development time. If it takes longer, you are building a product, not a micro-tool.
Focus on 'Read-Only' revenue: tools that process data are easier to maintain than tools that require constant content updates.
Leverage public build logs on Twitter/X to create a feedback loop that validates your product before it is even finished.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on one distribution channel (e.g., Product Hunt, Reddit, or direct outreach) to get your first 10 users, then iterate based on their feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Stop letting your projects die in the draft folder and start building assets that work for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
